2605.11136.title: EVOCHAMBER: Test-Time Co-evolution of Multi-Agent System at Individual, Team, and Population Scales
2605.11136.paper_summary: We argue that multi-agent test-time evolution is not single-agent evolution replicated N times. A single-agent learner can only evolve its own context and memory. A multi-agent system additionally evolves who collaborates, how they collaborate, and how knowledge flows across the population. These components have no single-agent counterpart and can produce phenomena such as emergent specialization. Yet prior test-time methods either confine experiences to individual agents, forfeiting cross-agent learning, or broadcast symmetrically to all agents, erasing the specialization that makes collaboration valuable. We present EVOCHAMBER, a training-free framework that instantiates test-time evolution at three levels over a coevolving agent pool. At its core is CODREAM (Collaborative Dreaming), a post-task protocol triggered on team failure or disagreement, in which agents collaboratively reflect, distill insights, and route them asymmetrically from strong to weak agents on the failed niche, preserving specialization while filling knowledge gaps. Team-level operators assemble niche-conditioned teams and select collaboration structures online. Population-level lifecycle operators fork, merge, prune, and seed agents under performance pressure. On three heterogeneous task streams with Qwen3-8B, EVOCHAMBER reaches 63.9% on competition math, 75.7% on code, and 87.1% on multi-domain reasoning, outperforming the best baseline by 32% relative on math and confirming asymmetric cross-agent transfer as the primary driver in ablation. Starting from several identically initialized agents, four to five stable niche specialists spontaneously emerge, a structural signature of multi-agent evolution that no single-agent learner can express. See our code at: https://github.com/Mercury7353/EvoChamber
2605.11136.paper_summary_zh: 我們主張，多智能體測試時演化並不是單一智能體演化重複 N 次。單一智能體學習者只能演化其自身的上下文和記憶。多智能體系統還會演化誰進行合作、他們如何合作，以及知識如何在整個群體中流動。這些組件沒有單一智能體的對應物，並且可以產生如新興專業化等現象。然而，先前的測試時方法要麼將經驗限制於個別智能體，放棄跨智能體學習，要麼對所有智能體進行對稱廣播，抹去使合作有價值的專業化。我們提出了 EVOCHAMBER，一個無需訓練的框架，實現了在共同演化的智能體池中進行三個層次的測試時演化。其核心是 CODREAM（協作夢想），這是一個在團隊失敗或意見不合時觸發的後任務協議，在此過程中，智能體共同反思、提煉見解，並將其不對稱地從強者路由到弱者，填補知識空白，同時保留專業化。團隊級操作員組建針對特定利基的團隊並在線選擇合作結構。群體級生命週期操作員在性能壓力下分叉、合併、修剪和播種智能體。在三個異質任務流上，使用 Qwen3-8B，EVOCHAMBER 在競賽數學上達到 63.9%、在代碼上達到 75.7%、在多領域推理上達到 87.1%，在數學上相對於最佳基線超越了 32%，並確認不對稱的跨智能體轉移是消融中的主要驅動力。從幾個初始化相同的智能體開始，自發出現四到五個穩定的利基專家，這是多智能體演化的結構特徵，單一智能體學習者無法表達。請查看我們的代碼： https://github.com/Mercury7353/EvoChamber
2605.11136.author: Yaolun Zhang et.al.
2605.11136.authors: Yaolun Zhang, Tianyi Xu, Shengyu Dai, Zhenwen Shao, Qingyun Wu, Huazheng Wang
